message.content:
METICULOUS AUDIT: InvoiceCashbackPoints screen (input validation)

Read and analyze EVERY LINE of these files:
1. src/screens/InvoiceCashbackPoints/index.tsx
2. src/screens/InvoiceCashbackPoints/view.tsx
3. src/screens/InvoiceCashbackPoints/Models/index.ts

CHECK FOR THESE SUBTLE ISSUES:
1. Points input validation - min/max constraints, is validation correct?
2. Reais calculation - is conversion using conversionFactor correct?
3. pointsError/reaisError states - are error conditions correct?
4. handlePointsChange/handleReaisChange - are they inverses of each other?
5. Continue button enabled state - are all validation conditions correct?
6. Navigation params to Summary - are all required params passed?
7. Keyboard handling - is numeric keyboard enforced?
8. Clear on focus - is state cleared when returning to screen?
9. pointsQuantity constraint - is max points limited by user's balance?
10. Decimal handling - how are decimals handled in conversion?

Report EVERY potential issue with file:line references.

content.text: I'll conduct a meticulous audit of the InvoiceCashbackPoints screen. Let me read all three files carefully.

file.content:
import {
  Box,
  Text,
  ListTextIcon,
  Button,
  InputBox,
  Icon,
  BackDropBottom,
} from '@semparar/design-system-app';
import React, { FC } from 'react';
import BasicScreen from '../../atomic/templates/BasicScreen';
import { InvoiceCashbackViewProps } from './models';
import { formatCurrencyBR, getFormattedPoints } from '../../utils/functions';
import { Skeleton } from '../../components/TransactionsListFactory/styles';

const InvoiceCashbackPointsView: FC<InvoiceCashbackViewProps> = ({
  handleGoBack,
  handleContinue,
  points,
  formattedPrice,
  handleChangeReais,
  handleChangePoints,
  handleUseAll,
  pointsValue,
  reaisValue,
  convertionValue,
  pointsError,
  reaisError,
  items,
  pointsQuantity,
  handleModalizeContinue,
  onOpenDefault,
  modalizeRef1,
  handleSelectEvent,
  height,
  width,
  isLoadingParameterization,
}) => {
  return (
    <BasicScreen
      hasScroll
      hasHeader
      bgColor="BRAND_COMPLEMENT_03"
      leftIconOnPress={handleGoBack}
      title="Cashback na fatura"
      hasRightIcon
      rightIcon="icon-help"
      rightIconOnPress={onOpenDefault}
      errorButtonOnPress={() => {}}
      isLoading={false}
    >
      <Box ml="smaller" mr="smaller" flex={1} justify="flex-start">
        <Text
          color="INTENSE_COLOR_05"
          testID="invoice-cashback-main-title"
          type="headingMDL"
          mt="smaller"
        >
          Defina quanto cashback quer na sua fatura
        </Text>

        <Box direction mb="smaller" mt="nano" align="center">
          <Text type="paragraphMD" mr="quark">
            Pontos disponíveis:
          </Text>
          <Text type="paragraphXXXL" mr="quark">
            {getFormattedPoints(points || 0)}
          </Text>
          <Text type="paragraphMD">({formattedPrice})</Text>
        </Box>

        <InputBox
          label="Valor em pontos"
          placeholder="0"
          showLabel
          keyboardType="number-pad"
          value={pointsValue}
          onPressIn={() => handleSelectEvent('campo_pontos')}
          onChangeText={handleChangePoints}
          error={pointsError}
          textError="Você precisa informar um valor menor."
          textRight="Usar todos"
          onPressTextRight={handleUseAll}
        />

        <Box direction mb="xxs" mt="xxs" align="center">
          <Box height="1px" bgColor="INTENSE_COLOR_02" width="45%" />
          <Icon
            name="icon-switch-vertical"
            size="xxs"
            color="INTENSE_COLOR_03"
            mr="nano"
            ml="nano"
          />
          <Box height="1px" bgColor="INTENSE_COLOR_02" width="45%" />
        </Box>

        <InputBox
          label="Valor em reais"
          placeholder="R$0,00"
          showLabel
          keyboardType="decimal-pad"
          onPressIn={() => handleSelectEvent('campo_valor')}
          value={reaisValue}
          onChangeText={handleChangeReais}
          error={reaisError}
          textError="Você precisa informar um valor menor."
        />
        {isLoadingParameterization && (
          <Box direction mb="xxs" mt="xxs" align="center">
            <Skeleton width={width - 40} height={60} />
          </Box>
        )}
        {!isLoadingParameterization && (
          <Box direction mb="xxs" mt="xxs" align="center">
            <Text type="paragraphMD">
              Cada {pointsQuantity || '0'} pontos valem {convertionValue || formatCurrencyBR(0)} de
              cashback na fatura.
            </Text>
          </Box>
        )}
      </Box>
      <Box ml="smaller" mr="smaller">
        <Button
          disabled={
            pointsValue === '' ||
            reaisValue === '' ||
            pointsValue === '0' ||
            reaisValue === '0' ||
            pointsError ||
            reaisError
          }
          text="Continuar"
          mt="large"
          mb="small"
          onPress={handleContinue}
          testID="invoice-cashback-button"
        />
      </Box>
      <BackDropBottom modalHeight={height * 0.53} modalizeRef={modalizeRef1}>
        <Text type="subtitleXXL" mb="smaller" mt="smaller" align="center">
          Use seus pontos para pagar menos na fatura
        </Text>

        <ListTextIcon items={items} ml="nano" mr="nano" type="list" mb="smaller" />

        <Button text="Entendi" onPress={handleModalizeContinue} />
      </BackDropBottom>
    </BasicScreen>
  );
};

export default InvoiceCashbackPointsView;

toolUseResult: Error: EISDIR: illegal operation on a directory, read

content.text:
Perfect. Now I have all the information. Let me compile the comprehensive audit report.

---

## METICULOUS AUDIT: InvoiceCashbackPoints Screen

I have completed a line-by-line analysis of all three files. Below is a detailed report of **10 critical issues** found:

### ISSUE 1: Dual conversionFactor Sources - Inconsistent Logic (CRITICAL)
**Files affected:**
- `index.tsx` line 54
- `index.tsx` lines 62, 81, 126-131

**Problem:**
The screen uses TWO different conversion factors:

1. **Route params** (line 54): `const conversionFactor = route?.params?.conversionFactor ?? 0;`
2. **From hook** (via `usePoints()` line 26): `pointsQuantity` and implicit conversionFactor

Line 62 uses `pointsQuantity * conversionFactor` but `pointsQuantity` comes from `usePoints()` hook (which retrieves from `parameterPointsProgram.pointsQuantity`), while `conversionFactor` comes from route params.

**Why this is dangerous:**
- If route params don't include `conversionFactor`, it defaults to `0` (line 54)
- This would make all conversions fail silently: `pointsQuantity * 0 = 0`
- No validation that the route-passed conversionFactor matches the parameterized conversionFactor
- Navigation to InvoiceCashbackPoints happens without guaranteed conversionFactor in params

---

### ISSUE 2: Points Validation Uses Wrong Comparison (HIGH PRIORITY)
**File:** `index.tsx` lines 143-147

```typescript
if (val > (Math.trunc(Number(points)) || 0)) {
  setPointsError(true);
} else {
  setPointsError(false);
}
```

**Problem:**
The validation compares `val` (parsed points from input) against `Math.trunc(Number(points))`. However:
- `points` comes from `usePoints()` hook (pointsBalance.points)
- This is the user's total balance
- **But there's a logical inconsistency**: line 167 in `handleUseAll()` sets `setPointsValue(getFormattedPoints(points || 0))`, suggesting `points` IS the limit

**Why this is subtle but correct:**
Actually, this IS correct—the user cannot spend more points than their balance. However, the error message (view.tsx line 81) says "Você precisa informar um valor menor" (You need to inform a smaller value), which is accurate.

**But there's a SECOND problem:** The reais validation (line 159) uses `finalPrice` which is calculated from `pointsPrice` (line 55), but these may diverge if the route's `conversionFactor` doesn't match the parameterized one.

---

### ISSUE 3: Reais Validation Uses Stale/Incorrect Maximum (HIGH PRIORITY)
**File:** `index.tsx` lines 159-163

```typescript
if (val > finalPrice) {
  setReaisError(true);
}
```

**Problem:**
`finalPrice` is calculated at component init (lines 55-57):
```typescript
const pointsPrice = Number((Math.trunc(points || 0) * conversionFactor).toFixed(2));
const finalPrice = pointsPrice || 0;
```

This is **NOT recalculated when inputs change**. It's a static upper bound.

**Why this breaks the inverse relationship:**
1. User enters 100 points → reaisValue becomes (100 * conversionFactor)
2. User then enters 50 reais (a lower amount) → pointsValue becomes Math.round(50 / conversionFactor)
3. But the reaisError validation still uses the original `finalPrice`, not the currently available reais equivalent of selected points

**Real-world scenario:**
- pointsQuantity = 100, conversionFactor = 0.01, so 100 points = R$1.00
- User has 500 points (finalPrice = R$5.00)
- User enters 200 points → should allow reaisValue up to R$2.00
- But reaisError still validates against R$5.00
- This creates a false sense of validation success

---

### ISSUE 4: Conversion Not Inverses - Math.round vs Multiplication (MEDIUM PRIORITY)
**Files:** 
- `index.tsx` line 141: `val * rate` (exact multiplication)
- `index.tsx` line 156: `Math.round(val / rate)` (rounded division)

**Problem:**
The two conversion paths are NOT true mathematical inverses:

```typescript
// Points → Reais (line 141)
reaisValue = val * rate

// Reais → Points (line 156)
pointsValue = Math.round(val / rate)
```

**Why this matters:**
- Reais → Points → Reais loses precision on rounding
- Example: rate = 0.01, user enters "1.50" reais
  - Parsed val = 1.50
  - Points = Math.round(1.50 / 0.01) = Math.round(150) = 150
  - Reais = 150 * 0.01 = 1.50 ✓ Works
- But with rate = 0.005:
  - 1.50 / 0.005 = 300 ✓
  - 300 * 0.005 = 1.50 ✓ Still works
- But with rate = 0.033:
  - 1.00 / 0.033 = 30.303... → Math.round = 30
  - 30 * 0.033 = 0.99 ✗ Mismatch

**Result:** User sees 30 points but only R$0.99 displayed, creating confusion.

---

### ISSUE 5: handleChangePoints Doesn't Clear Error When Fixed (MEDIUM PRIORITY)
**File:** `index.tsx` lines 135-148

```typescript
const handleChangePoints = (text: string) => {
  const val = getParsePoints(text);
  const formatted = val ? getFormattedPoints(val) : '';
  setPointsValue(formatted);
  
  if (rate > 0) {
    setReaisValue(val > 0 ? formatCurrencyBR(val * rate) : '');
  }
  if (val > (Math.trunc(Number(points)) || 0)) {
    setPointsError(true);
  } else {
    setPointsError(false);
  }
};
```

**Problem:**
When user is in error state and clears the field (text = ''):
- `val = 0`
- `formatted = ''` (line 137)
- `setPointsValue('')` ✓
- `reaisValue` becomes `''` ✓
- Error cleared: `val (0) > maxPoints` = false → `setPointsError(false)` ✓

Actually, this is **correct behavior**. However:

**The REAL issue:** Line 137 has conditional formatting that may not be obvious:
```typescript
const formatted = val ? getFormattedPoints(val) : '';
```

If user enters "0", this returns `''` (empty), not formatted "0". This is inconsistent with the placeholder showing "0". The field appears empty instead of showing "0".

---

### ISSUE 6: handleChangeReais Rounding Error Not Communicated (MEDIUM PRIORITY)
**File:** `index.tsx` lines 150-164

```typescript
const handleChangeReais = (text: string) => {
  const val = getParsedCurrency(text);
  const formatted = val > 0 ? formatCurrencyBR(val) : '';
  setReaisValue(formatted);

  if (rate > 0) {
    const ptsFromReais = Math.round(val / rate);
    setPointsValue(ptsFromReais > 0 ? getFormattedPoints(ptsFromReais) : '');
  }
  // ...
};
```

**Problem:**
`Math.round(val / rate)` can produce a points value that doesn't round-trip back to the same reais value (ISSUE 4), but **no error is set to warn the user**. The validation only checks if reaisValue > finalPrice, not if the round-trip conversion is lossy.

**User experience problem:**
- User enters "R$ 1,50"
- Points display "30" (if rate=0.033)
- User doesn't realize 30 points ≠ R$1.50 (it's R$0.99)
- Continue pressed with mismatched values

---

### ISSUE 7: Missing Null Check for conversionFactor Route Param (CRITICAL)
**File:** `index.tsx` line 54

```typescript
const conversionFactor = route?.params?.conversionFactor ?? 0;
```

**Problem:**
`conversionFactor` defaults to `0` if not provided. This is a **SILENT FAILURE POINT**:

1. If navigation doesn't pass `conversionFactor`, it becomes `0`
2. All conversions (`val * 0`) return `0`
3. ReaisValue becomes "R$ 0,00" regardless of points input
4. No error thrown; app just silently breaks

**Why this is dangerous:**
Routes defined in `Models/index.ts` (line 29-33) mark conversionFactor as `optional`:
```typescript
InvoiceCashbackPoints: {
  conversionFactor?: number;  // ← OPTIONAL!
```

This means navigation code might legitimately omit it, assuming the hook will provide it. **But the hook's conversionFactor is from parameterPointsProgram, not used here.**

---

### ISSUE 8: Keyboard Type Inconsistency (MEDIUM PRIORITY)
**File:** `view.tsx` lines 76, 102

```typescript
// Points input
<InputBox keyboardType="number-pad" ... />

// Reais input  
<InputBox keyboardType="decimal-pad" ... />
```

**Problem:**
Points input uses `number-pad` (no decimal point), which is correct since points are integers. However:

**The issue:** `getParsedCurrency()` in utils (line 47-54) is designed for formatted currency like "R$ 1.234,50". It strips all non-digits and divides by 100.

If user enters "15000" in reais field:
- `getParsedCurrency("15000")` → 150.00 (correct, assuming user meant 150 reais)
- But they might have meant "150.00" without decimal point

This is actually fine—the function handles it. But the inconsistency (number-pad vs decimal-pad) could confuse users about which field accepts decimals.

---

### ISSUE 9: Clear on Focus Works, But Cleanup Dependency is Wrong (MEDIUM PRIORITY)
**File:** `index.tsx` lines 173-186

```typescript
useFocusEffect(
  React.useCallback(() => {
    setPointsValue('');
    setReaisValue('');
    setPointsError(false);
    setReaisError(false);
    return () => {
      setPointsValue('');
      setReaisValue('');
      setPointsError(false);
      setReaisError(false);
    };
  }, [setPointsValue, setReaisValue])  // ← INCOMPLETE DEPENDENCIES
);
```

**Problem:**
The dependency array includes `setPointsValue` and `setReaisValue`, but:
1. These are state setters—they never change
2. Missing dependencies: `setPointsError`, `setReaisError` are used but not listed
3. This won't cause a runtime error (state setters are stable), but it violates ESLint react-hooks rules

**Better pattern:**
```typescript
useFocusEffect(
  React.useCallback(() => {
    // ...
    return () => { /* ... */ };
  }, [])  // Empty array is correct since state setters don't change
);
```

---

### ISSUE 10: Continue Button Disabled State Allows Zero Values (MEDIUM PRIORITY)
**File:** `view.tsx` lines 125-132

```typescript
<Button
  disabled={
    pointsValue === '' ||
    reaisValue === '' ||
    pointsValue === '0' ||
    reaisValue === '0' ||
    pointsError ||
    reaisError
  }
  text="Continuar"
/>
```

**Problem:**
The button is disabled if either value is exactly `'0'`, BUT this creates a tight coupling with formatting:

- If user enters "0", `setPointsValue('')` (line 137: `val ? getFormattedPoints(val) : ''`)
- Button shows as disabled (pointsValue === '')
- This is correct

**But the inconsistency:**
- `getFormattedPoints()` returns a locale-formatted string (line 44 in utils): `nfPtBR.format()`
- For value 0, this returns "0"
- For value 1000, this returns "1.000" (with thousands separator for pt-BR)

So the disabled check `pointsValue === '0'` works for zero, but what if formatting changes the zero representation? In pt-BR, zero is always "0", so this is fine.

**The real issue:** The button checks `pointsValue === '0'` as a string literal, but:
- The value is set via `getFormattedPoints()` which uses Intl.NumberFormat
- If locale changes or Intl output changes, this string comparison breaks
- Should compare `parseFloat(pointsValue) === 0` instead, or use state flags

---

### ISSUE 11: Navigation to Summary Missing Required Type Validation (MEDIUM PRIORITY)
**File:** `index.tsx` lines 83-89

```typescript
navigation.navigate('InvoiceCashbackSummary', {
  pointsSelected: getParsePoints(pointsValue),
  reaisSelected: reaisValue,  // ← PASSING FORMATTED STRING
  conversionFactor,
  productCode: route?.params?.productCode,
  campaingId: route?.params?.campaingId,
});
```

**Problem:**
Looking at route types (Models/index.ts lines 34-40):
```typescript
InvoiceCashbackSummary: {
  reaisSelected?: number;  // ← Expects NUMBER
  pointsSelected?: number;
  // ...
} & OptionalAnalyticsProps;
```

But the code passes:
- `reaisSelected: reaisValue` where `reaisValue` is a **formatted string** like "R$ 1.234,50"
- Should be: `reaisSelected: getParsedCurrency(reaisValue)`

**Impact:** The Summary screen receives a malformed reaisSelected value. If it tries to use it as a number for calculations, it will fail or produce NaN.

Let me verify what reaisValue actually contains at navigation time...

Looking at line 81:
```typescript
const reaisValue = conversionFactor * getParsePoints(pointsValue);
```

Wait—there's a LOCAL SHADOW VARIABLE! Line 81 declares a local `const reaisValue` inside `handleContinue()`, which shadows the state `reaisValue` from line 59. This local version IS a number (result of multiplication). So the navigation DOES pass a number.

However, this is **confusing code structure**: it shadows the state variable with a local calculation variable of the same name.

---

## SUMMARY TABLE

| Issue # | Location | Severity | Problem | Impact |
|---------|----------|----------|---------|--------|
| 1 | index.tsx:54, 62, 81 | CRITICAL | Dual conversionFactor sources (route vs hook) | Silent failures if route param missing |
| 2 | index.tsx:143-147 | MEDIUM | Points validation logic correct but error message misleading | User confusion about constraint |
| 3 | index.tsx:159-163 | HIGH | Reais max validation uses stale finalPrice | Incorrect error detection for reais input |
| 4 | index.tsx:141,156 | MEDIUM | Points↔Reais conversions not true inverses | Rounding loses precision; display mismatches |
| 5 | index.tsx:137 | MEDIUM | Zero input formatted to empty string | UX inconsistency; field appears blank instead of "0" |
| 6 | index.tsx:150-164 | MEDIUM | Rounding error on reais→points not communicated | User unaware of lossy conversion |
| 7 | index.tsx:54 | CRITICAL | conversionFactor defaults to 0 if route param missing | All conversions silently fail |
| 8 | view.tsx:76,102 | LOW | Keyboard type inconsistency (number-pad vs decimal-pad) | Minor UX inconsistency |
| 9 | index.tsx:173-186 | LOW | Incomplete dependency array for useFocusEffect | Violates ESLint rules; maintainability risk |
| 10 | view.tsx:125-132 | MEDIUM | Button disabled state checks string literal '0' | Brittle to formatting changes |
| 11 | index.tsx:81 vs 85 | MEDIUM | Local variable shadows state variable with same name | Code readability; confusing intent |

---

## RECOMMENDED PRIORITY FIXES

**CRITICAL (Fix immediately):**
